title: Orbán’s still winning over older rural voters — but not the young
summary: Campaigning on the risks of being sucked into the neighboring war in Ukraine is working like a spell on the elderly in Fidesz’s heartlands.

title: The great Russian disconnect
summary: Silicon Valley is pulling the plug. The Kremlin is locking the doors. Inside the race to cut Russia off from the global internet — and what comes after.

title: EU agrees on rollover of Russia sanctions
summary: Two names were removed from the list to secure an agreement on Saturday.

title: ‘Isolated’ Slovakia drops EU veto threat on Russia sanctions
summary: Slovakia had pushed for the EU to delist two Russian oligarchs, but did a U-turn on Saturday.

title: Interview: Zelensky’s advisor says more weapons, sanctions and pressure on financial sector could bring Russia down
summary: We spoke with president Volodymyr Zelensky’s adviser about how he sees the dispute over the Druzhba pipeline, what motivated Russia to attack the pumping station in Brody; why Robert Fico and Viktor Orbán are so obsessed with buying Russian energy, why there should be far more attacks on Russian tankers, and the impact of the war against Iran on developments in Ukraine.

title: EU foreign ministers face tense talks on Ukraine amid spillover fears of Iran war
summary: Amid concerns about waning US aid and shortages of air‑defence systems for Ukraine linked to the Iran war, there are expected to be renewed calls during Monday's foreign affairs council meeting for increased bilateral military support for Kyiv alongside the stalled €90bn in EU funding that Hungary is currently blocking.

title: Ukraine Battlefield update (day 1,478): Why Ukraine is more optimistic than last year ahead of Russian spring offensive
summary: Ukraine managed to cut off the Russians from the US Starlink satellite system and increased drone attacks against Russia's rear positions.

title: 31 days to election: Orbán claims Ukrainians are threatening his children and grandchildren
summary: In our regular digest of the week’s news from the Hungarian campaign trail: latest opinion polls, a Hungarian delegation to Ukraine to inspect the damaged Russian oil pipeline, claims Kremlin-approved Russian disinfo is amplifying Fidesz campaign messaging, Hollywood star Mark Ruffalo calls on Hungarians to free themselves from Orban, and physical attacks on independent journalists.

title: Ukraine Battlefield update (Day 1,477): ‘I don’t want to any more, but I still have to’ – what I heard from a Ukrainian soldier
summary: Ukrainian anti-tank ditches contain barbed wire, between them rows of dragon’s teeth connected by steel cables, and between those further belts of barbed wire. Parts of the ditches were flooded by melted snow.

title: Russian fossil-fuel daily revenues up 14% since start of US-Israel attack on Iran
summary: Russia has earned an estimated €6bn in fossil-fuel, or €510m per day, since the attacks on Iran, according to a new analysis published on Thursday.

title: What Sarkozy’s corruption appeal means for cleaning up European politics
summary: It is easy to treat the case as just a French affair. In reality, the appeal carries wider international stakes and has become a stress test for the rule-of-law ecosystem under pressure across the EU.

title: Russia-linked priests preaching Azerbaijan war, Armenia warns EU
summary: “One of the archbishops in Armenia had started a movement to [reactivate] ... the [Nagorno-Karabakh] conflict”, warned Armenian prime minister Nikol Pashinyan.

title: Arms flows rise to Cold War levels amid Ukraine and EU rearmament
summary: Global arms flows are surging to Cold War levels, fuelled by European rearmament and the war in Ukraine. Ukraine, Poland, and the Czech Republic are reshaping Europe’s defence landscape, while a Czechoslovak Group’s record-breaking IPO exposes links between business and politics. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia eyes Ukrainian weapons amid drone threats, highlighting a defence boom that is both highly profitable and increasingly entangled with geopolitics.

title: Ukraine and allies fear easing Russian sanctions will prolong war
summary: Ukraine's president says the US decision "certainly does not help achieve peace".

title: Why has Trump eased sanctions on Russian oil - and will it help Putin?
summary: The US said easing sanctions on Russian oil would provide only a limited financial boost to Putin.

title: Trial starts in case of explosives sent to UK and Poland
summary: The parcel plot is one of a wave of sabotage attacks across Europe which officials have linked to Russia.

title: War in Ukraine spills into Hungarian election campaign
summary: Hungary is going to the polls soon and Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has one public enemy in his sights - Ukraine's president.

title: Zelensky sends drone teams to Middle East, touting Ukraine's expertise
summary: The Ukrainian president said it was a "good feeling" when allies asked for help countering drones.

title: Steve Rosenberg: Russia seeks diplomatic and economic gains from Iran war
summary: President Putin pits himself as a potential mediator but that's not an easy sell, writes the BBC's Russia editor.

title: Zelenskyy decries ‘blackmail’ by Europe over Druzhba oil pipeline
summary: The pipeline has been offline since January after a Russian attack, and has been at the center of a bitter row between Ukraine and Hungary.

title: Iran says Russia and China providing ‘military cooperation’
summary: Tehran has had "good cooperation with these countries: politically, economically, even militarily," Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi told MS NOW.

title: Was folgt aus dem Iran-Krieg für Ukraine und Europa? Mit Florence Gaub
summary: Zwei Wochen nach Beginn der US-Invasion im Iran treten die massiven globalen Nebenwirkungen zutage. Während Donald Trump durch die Lockerung der Sanktionen gegen russisches Öl die Ukraine-Front schwächt, entstehen im Nahen Osten völlig neue Zweckbündnisse. Gordon Repinski analysiert gemeinsam mit der Strategie-Expertin Florence Gaub (NATO Defense College), warum die USA derzeit eher operationell getrieben als […]
